user.title: 📡 Offline Devices
user.description: Track Home Assistant devices whose entities have all become unavailable. One instance covers every device; ZHA and Matter get their own breakdown entities.

abort.already_configured: Offline Devices is already set up.

data.enable_repairs: 🛠️ Create repair issues for offline devices

data_description.enable_repairs: Off by default. When on, raises one repair issue per offline device, linking to the device, its integration, and — for ZHA — the add-Zigbee-device screen.
data_description.ignored_names: Devices whose name contains any of these (case-insensitive) are never reported. The 'intermittent' device-registry label is always honored too.

confirm.title: 📡 {name} is offline
confirm.description:
All entities of **{name}** ({area}) are unavailable.

- [Open the device]({device_link})
- [Open its integration]({integration_link})

Reconnect or power-cycle the device. Submitting dismisses this issue; it returns automatically if the device stays offline.

confirm.title: 📡 ZHA device {name} is offline
confirm.description:
All entities of the Zigbee device **{name}** ({area}) are unavailable.

- [Open the device]({device_link})
- [Re-pair a Zigbee device]({zha_add_link})

Try power-cycling the device or re-pairing it. Submitting dismisses this issue; it returns automatically if the device stays offline.

confirm.title: 📡 Matter device {name} is offline
confirm.description:
All entities of the Matter device **{name}** ({area}) are unavailable.

- [Open the device]({device_link})
- [Open the Matter integration]({integration_link})

Reconnect or power-cycle the device. Submitting dismisses this issue; it returns automatically if the device stays offline.
